subject: Accounting
title: Source Documents
description: A quick, practical guide to Source Documents.

hook.text: No receipt, no entry. Every bookkeeping record needs a source document to prove it happened.

buildup.text: Invoices, receipts, bank statements, contracts—these are the evidence behind every transaction.

discovery.text: Source documents provide date, amount, parties involved, and the nature of the transaction.

twist.text: Lost documents can make audits impossible and tax deductions unclaimable. Filing matters.

climax.text: Digital bookkeeping has replaced shoeboxes of receipts—but the principle remains: document everything.

punchline.text: If you can't prove it happened, it didn't happen.

quiz.question: Why are source documents important in bookkeeping?
options: They prove that transactions actually occurred
They replace the need for accounting
